content.content:
1→{
     2→  config,
     3→  pkgs,
     4→  lib,
     5→  ...
     6→}:
     7→
     8→{
     9→  imports = [
    10→    ./hypridle.nix
    11→    ./hyprlock.nix
    12→    ./hyprpaper.nix
    13→    ./mako.nix
    14→    ./waybar.nix
    15→  ];
    16→
    17→  home.packages = with pkgs; [
    18→    grim # Screenshot tool
    19→    slurp # Region selector
    20→    swappy # Snapshot editor
    21→    wl-clipboard # Clipboard manager
    22→    wf-recorder # Screen recorder
    23→    ffmpeg # For GIF conversion
    24→    jq # JSON processor
    25→
    26→    # Script to record GIF
    27→    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "record-gif" ''
    28→      #!/usr/bin/env bash
    29→      # Define output
    30→      DATE=$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S)
    31→      DIR="$HOME/Pictures/GIFs"
    32→      MKV="$DIR/recording_$DATE.mkv"
    33→      GIF="$DIR/recording_$DATE.gif"
    34→
    35→      mkdir -p "$DIR"
    36→
    37→      # Notify and Select Region
    38→      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Select region to start recording..."
    39→
    40→      if ! REGION=$(slurp); then
    41→          notify-send "GIF Recording" "Cancelled"
    42→          exit 1
    43→      fi
    44→
    45→      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Recording started! Press Super+Shift+S to stop."
    46→
    47→      # Record to MKV (more robust than MP4 for interruption)
    48→      wf-recorder -g "$REGION" -f "$MKV"
    49→
    50→      # Convert to GIF after recording stops
    51→      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Converting to GIF..."
    52→
    53→      # Generate palette for better quality
    54→      palette="/tmp/palette.png"
    55→      # higher quality scale
    56→      filters="fps=15,scale=720:-1:flags=lanczos"
    57→
    58→      ffmpeg -v warning -i "$MKV" -vf "$filters,palettegen" -y "$palette"
    59→      ffmpeg -v warning -i "$MKV" -i "$palette" -lavfi "$filters [x]; [x][1:v] paletteuse" -y "$GIF"
    60→
    61→      rm "$palette"
    62→      rm "$MKV"
    63→
    64→      notify-send "GIF Recording" "Saved to $GIF"
    65→      wl-copy "$GIF"
    66→    '')
    67→
    68→    # Screenshot scripts (avoids quoting issues in Hyprland binds)
    69→    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "screenshot-region" ''
    70→      REGION=$(slurp) || exit 0
    71→      grim -g "$REGION" - | wl-copy
    72→    '')
    73→    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "screenshot-region-edit" ''
    74→      REGION=$(slurp) || exit 0
    75→      grim -g "$REGION" - | swappy -f -
    76→    '')
    77→
    78→    # Cheatsheet Script
    79→    (pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "hypr-cheatsheet" ''
    80→      #!/usr/bin/env bash
    81→      # Parse keybindings from hyprctl using structured JSON
    82→      hyprctl binds -j | jq -r '
    83→        .[]
    84→        | select(.has_description and .description != "")
    85→        | (
    86→            (if .modmask == 0 then ""
    87→             else [
    88→               (if (.modmask % 128) >= 64 then "Super" else empty end),
    89→               (if (.modmask % 2) >= 1 then "Shift" else empty end),
    90→               (if (.modmask % 8) >= 4 then "Ctrl" else empty end),
    91→               (if (.modmask % 16) >= 8 then "Alt" else empty end)
    92→             ] | join(" + ")
    93→             end)
    94→          ) as $mods
    95→        | (if $mods == "" then .key else $mods + " + " + .key end)
    96→          + " — " + .description
    97→      ' | fuzzel --dmenu --width 80 --lines 20 --prompt "Keybindings: "
    98→    '')
    99→  ];
   100→
   101→  wayland.windowManager.hyprland = {
   102→    enable = true;
   103→    settings = {
   104→      # --- Monitors ---
   105→      # Auto-detect monitors with preferred resolution
   106→      # Format: name, resolution, position, scale
   107→      monitor = [
   108→        "DP-2, highrr, auto, 1"
   109→        ", preferred, auto, 1"
   110→      ];
   111→
   112→      # --- Workspace Layouts ---
   113→      workspace = [
   114→        "1, layout:scrolling"
   115→        "2, layout:master"
   116→        "3, layout:master"
   117→        "4, layout:master"
   118→        "5, layout:dwindle"
   119→      ];
   120→
   121→      # --- Input ---
   122→      input = {
   123→        kb_layout = "us";
   124→        follow_mouse = 1;
   125→        sensitivity = 0; # -1.0 - 1.0, 0 means no modification.
   126→        natural_scroll = true;
   127→      };
   128→
   129→      # --- General ---
   130→      general = {
   131→        gaps_in = 5;
   132→        gaps_out = 10;
   133→        border_size = 2;
   134→        "col.active_border" = "rgb(cba6f7) rgb(94e2d5) 45deg"; # Catppuccin Mocha Mauve -> Teal
   135→        "col.inactive_border" = "rgb(585b70)"; # Catppuccin Mocha Surface2
   136→        layout = "master";
   137→      };
   138→
   139→      # --- Master Layout ---
   140→      master = {
   141→        new_status = "slave";
   142→        orientation = "left";
   143→        mfact = 0.55;
   144→      };
   145→
   146→      # --- Scrolling Layout ---
   147→      scrolling = {
   148→        column_width = "0.3";
   149→        explicit_column_widths = "0.25, 0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.667, 1.0";
   150→      };
   151→
   152→      # --- Decoration ---
   153→      decoration = {
   154→        rounding = 10;
   155→        blur = {
   156→          enabled = true;
   157→          size = 3;
   158→          passes = 1;
   159→        };
   160→        shadow = {
   161→          enabled = true;
   162→          range = 4;
   163→          render_power = 3;
   164→          color = "rgba(1e1e2eee)"; # Catppuccin Mocha Base
   165→        };
   166→      };
   167→
   168→      # --- Cursor ---
   169→      cursor = {
   170→        no_hardware_cursors = true;
   171→      };
   172→
   173→      # --- Layer Rules ---
   174→      layerrule = [
   175→        "blur on, ignore_alpha 1, match:namespace waybar"
   176→        "blur on, ignore_alpha 1, match:namespace notifications"
   177→      ];
   178→
   179→      # --- Window Rules ---
   180→      # Hide Chrome's Spotify "now playing" floating popup (redundant with waybar mpris)
   181→      windowrule = [
   182→        "match:class ^(google-chrome)$, match:float yes, match:title .*•.*, workspace special:trash silent"
   183→      ];
   184→
   185→      # --- Keybindings ---
   186→      # NOTE: Keyboard is a Kinesis Advantage (split ergo). Super, Enter, Space,
   187→      # Ctrl(R), PgUp, PgDn are all on the right thumb cluster. Avoid chords
   188→      # combining any two of these keys (e.g. Super+Return, Super+Space).
   189→      # Super+Backspace works (Backspace is on the left thumb cluster).
   190→      #
   191→      # Directional home row keys: J=left, K=down, L=up, ;=right
   192→      # (Kinesis Advantage key wells make JKL; more natural than HJKL)
   193→      #
   194→      # Modifier scheme for JKL;:
   195→      #   Super       = focus (spatial, works across all layouts)
   196→      #   Super+Shift = move/swap window
   197→      #   Super+Ctrl  = resize
   198→      #   Super+Alt   = group/tab cycling
   199→      "$mainMod" = "SUPER";
   200→      bindd = [
   201→        "$mainMod, Q, Open terminal, exec, kitty"
   202→        "$mainMod, C, Close window, killactive,"
   203→        "$mainMod, M, Lock screen, exec, hyprlock"
   204→        "$mainMod, E, File manager, exec, thunar"
   205→        "$mainMod, Y, Terminal file manager, exec, kitty -e yazi"
   206→        "$mainMod, B, Open browser, exec, google-chrome-stable"
   207→        "$mainMod, V, Toggle floating, togglefloating,"
   208→        "$mainMod, F, Toggle fullscreen, fullscreen, 1"
   209→        "$mainMod SHIFT, F, True fullscreen, fullscreen, 0"
   210→        "$mainMod, R, App launcher, exec, fuzzel"
   211→        "$mainMod, slash, Keybindings cheatsheet, exec, hypr-cheatsheet"
   212→        "$mainMod, P, Pseudotile, pseudo,"
   213→
   214→        # Focus
   215→        "$mainMod, left, Focus left, movefocus, l"
   216→        "$mainMod, right, Focus right, movefocus, r"
   217→        "$mainMod, up, Focus up, movefocus, u"
   218→        "$mainMod, down, Focus down, movefocus, d"
   219→
   220→        # Focus (home row — spatial movefocus works across all layouts)
   221→        "$mainMod, J, Focus left, movefocus, l"
   222→        "$mainMod, K, Focus down, movefocus, d"
   223→        "$mainMod, L, Focus up, movefocus, u"
   224→        "$mainMod, semicolon, Focus right, movefocus, r"
   225→
   226→        # Move window
   227→        "$mainMod SHIFT, left, Move window left, movewindow, l"
   228→        "$mainMod SHIFT, right, Move window right, movewindow, r"
   229→        "$mainMod SHIFT, up, Move window up, movewindow, u"
   230→        "$mainMod SHIFT, down, Move window down, movewindow, d"
   231→
   232→        # Move window (home row)
   233→        "$mainMod SHIFT, J, Move window left, movewindow, l"
   234→        "$mainMod SHIFT, K, Move window down, movewindow, d"
   235→        "$mainMod SHIFT, L, Move window up, movewindow, u"
   236→        "$mainMod SHIFT, semicolon, Move window right, movewindow, r"
   237→
   238→        # Master layout
   239→        "$mainMod, BackSpace, Swap with master, layoutmsg, swapwithmaster master"
   240→        "$mainMod SHIFT, BackSpace, Focus master, layoutmsg, focusmaster auto"
   241→        "$mainMod, comma, Add master window, layoutmsg, addmaster"
   242→        "$mainMod, period, Remove master window, layoutmsg, removemaster"
   243→
   244→        # Scroll layout (workspace 1)
   245→        "$mainMod, equal, Fit visible columns, layoutmsg, fit visible"
   246→        "$mainMod SHIFT, equal, Cycle column width, layoutmsg, colresize +conf"
   247→
   248→        # Workspaces
   249→        "$mainMod, 1, Workspace 1, workspace, 1"
   250→        "$mainMod, 2, Workspace 2, workspace, 2"
   251→        "$mainMod, 3, Workspace 3, workspace, 3"
   252→        "$mainMod, 4, Workspace 4, workspace, 4"
   253→        "$mainMod, 5, Workspace 5, workspace, 5"
   254→
   255→        # Move to workspace
   256→        "$mainMod SHIFT, 1, Move to workspace 1, movetoworkspace, 1"
   257→        "$mainMod SHIFT, 2, Move to workspace 2, movetoworkspace, 2"
   258→        "$mainMod SHIFT, 3, Move to workspace 3, movetoworkspace, 3"
   259→        "$mainMod SHIFT, 4, Move to workspace 4, movetoworkspace, 4"
   260→        "$mainMod SHIFT, 5, Move to workspace 5, movetoworkspace, 5"
   261→
   262→        # Screenshots
   263→        ", Print, Screenshot region to clipboard, exec, screenshot-region"
   264→        "$mainMod, Print, Screenshot region to editor, exec, screenshot-region-edit"
   265→        "SHIFT, Print, Screenshot fullscreen, exec, grim - | wl-copy"
   266→
   267→        # Screenshot alternatives (no Print key)
   268→        "$mainMod SHIFT, P, Screenshot region to clipboard, exec, screenshot-region"
   269→        "$mainMod ALT, P, Screenshot region to editor, exec, screenshot-region-edit"
   270→        "$mainMod CTRL, P, Screenshot fullscreen, exec, grim - | wl-copy"
   271→
   272→        # Screen recording
   273→        "$mainMod SHIFT, G, Record GIF, exec, record-gif"
   274→        "$mainMod SHIFT, S, Stop recording, exec, pkill --signal SIGINT wf-recorder"
   275→
   276→        # Workspace cycling
   277→        "$mainMod, Tab, Next workspace, workspace, m+1"
   278→        "$mainMod SHIFT, Tab, Previous workspace, workspace, m-1"
   279→
   280→        # Urgent
   281→        "$mainMod, U, Focus urgent window, focusurgentorlast,"
   282→
   283→        # Groups (tabbed windows)
   284→        "$mainMod, G, Toggle group, togglegroup,"
   285→        "$mainMod ALT, J, Change active in group backward, changegroupactive, b"
   286→        "$mainMod ALT, semicolon, Change active in group forward, changegroupactive, f"
   287→
   288→        # Notifications
   289→        "$mainMod SHIFT, N, Restore notification, exec, makoctl restore"
   290→        "$mainMod, N, Dismiss notification, exec, makoctl dismiss"
   291→      ];
   292→
   293→      # --- Mouse Bindings ---
   294→      bindm = [
   295→        "ALT, mouse:272, movewindow"
   296→        "ALT, mouse:273, resizewindow"
   297→      ];
   298→
   299→      # --- Keyboard Resize (Super+Ctrl+JKL;) ---
   300→      binde = [
   301→        "$mainMod CTRL, J, resizeactive, -20 0"
   302→        "$mainMod CTRL, K, resizeactive, 0 20"
   303→        "$mainMod CTRL, L, resizeactive, 0 -20"
   304→        "$mainMod CTRL, semicolon, resizeactive, 20 0"
   305→      ];
   306→
   307→      debug = {
   308→        disable_logs = false;
   309→      };
   310→    };
   311→  };
   312→
   313→  systemd.user.targets.hyprland-session = {
   314→    Unit = {
   315→      Description = lib.mkForce "Hyprland session";
   316→      BindsTo = [ "graphical-session.target" ];
   317→      Wants = [ "graphical-session.target" ];
   318→      After = [ "graphical-session.target" ];
   319→    };
   320→  };
   321→}
   322→

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - 0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake: Community-driven Nix Flake for the Zen browser · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake"},{"title":"GitHub - MarceColl/zen-browser-flake: Nix Flake for the Zen Browser · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/MarceColl/zen-browser-flake"},{"title":"GitHub - youwen5/zen-browser-flake: Nix Flake for the Zen Browser, with automatic updates","url":"https://github.com/youwen5/zen-browser-flake"},{"title":"Installing Zen Browser on NixOS ❄️ | by quiet | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@notquitethereyet_/installing-zen-browser-on-nixos-%EF%B8%8F-7ae541f5533f"},{"title":"GitHub - youwen5/zen-browser-source-flake: flake for building zen browser from source","url":"https://github.com/youwen5/zen-browser-source-flake"},{"title":"GitHub - sandptel/zen-browser-flake-new: Nix Flake for the Zen Browser, with automatic updates · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/sandptel/zen-browser-flake-new"},{"title":"zen-browser-flake/flake.nix at main · 0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake","url":"https://github.com/0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake/blob/main/flake.nix"},{"title":"zen-browser-flake/README.md at master · MarceColl/zen-browser-flake","url":"https://github.com/MarceColl/zen-browser-flake/blob/master/README.md"},{"title":"Zen Browser - Official NixOS Wiki","url":"https://wiki.nixos.org/wiki/Zen_Browser"},{"title":"GitHub - NikSneMC/zen-browser-flake","url":"https://github.com/NikSneMC/zen-browser-flake"}]

Based on my search results, here are the main zen-browser-flake projects on GitHub:

## Primary zen-browser-flake Repositories

1. **0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake - Community-driven Nix Flake for the Zen browser**
   - GitHub URL: `github:0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake`
   - Supports home-manager and nixpkgs integration

2. **youwen5/zen-browser-flake - Originally forked from the unmaintained MarceColl/zen-browser-flake, but heavily modified**
   - GitHub URL: `github:youwen5/zen-browser-flake`
   - Has more correct packaging that closely mirrors how Firefox is packaged in nixpkgs, using wrapFirefox
   - GitHub Actions is configured to automatically check for updates daily

3. **MarceColl/zen-browser-flake**
   - GitHub URL: `github:MarceColl/zen-browser-flake`
   - Exposes two packages, corresponding to the specific and generic zen versions

4. **NikSneMC/zen-browser-flake - Exposes three channels, alpha, beta and twilight, and every version of the Zen browser**
   - GitHub URL: `github:NikSneMC/zen-browser-flake`

The most commonly recommended options appear to be the `youwen5` fork (for better packaging) and the `0xc000022070` community-driven version.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "Zen Browser Nix flake github"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - 0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake: Community-driven Nix Flake for the Zen browser · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake"},{"title":"GitHub - MarceColl/zen-browser-flake: Nix Flake for the Zen Browser · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/MarceColl/zen-browser-flake"},{"title":"GitHub - youwen5/zen-browser-flake: Nix Flake for the Zen Browser, with automatic updates","url":"https://github.com/youwen5/zen-browser-flake"},{"title":"Installing Zen Browser on NixOS ❄️ | by quiet | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@notquitethereyet_/installing-zen-browser-on-nixos-%EF%B8%8F-7ae541f5533f"},{"title":"GitHub - sandptel/zen-browser-flake-new: Nix Flake for the Zen Browser, with automatic updates · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/sandptel/zen-browser-flake-new"},{"title":"Zen Browser - Official NixOS Wiki","url":"https://wiki.nixos.org/w/index.php?title=Zen_Browser&mobileaction=toggle_view_desktop"},{"title":"GitHub - jasper-clarke/zen-browser-nixos: A flake for NixOS which provides Zen Browser at it's most recent release","url":"https://github.com/jasper-clarke/zen-browser-nixos"},{"title":"GitHub - youwen5/zen-browser-source-flake: flake for building zen browser from source","url":"https://github.com/youwen5/zen-browser-source-flake"},{"title":"GitHub - myamusashi/zen-twilight-flake: Nix Flake for the Zen Browser","url":"https://github.com/myamusashi/zen-twilight-flake"},{"title":"GitHub - zstg/zen-browser: Zen browser packaged for NixOS","url":"https://github.com/zstg/zen-browser"}]

Based on the search results, here are several Zen Browser Nix flakes available on GitHub:

## Popular Zen Browser Nix Flakes

1. **0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake - A community-driven Nix Flake for the Zen browser**. This flake can be added to your NixOS configuration by using the input URL: github:0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake.

2. **youwen5/zen-browser-flake - A Nix Flake for the Zen Browser with automatic updates, originally forked from the unmaintained MarceColl/zen-browser-flake, with GitHub Actions configured to automatically check for updates daily**.

3. **MarceColl/zen-browser-flake - A Nix Flake for the Zen Browser that exposes two packages corresponding to specific and generic zen versions**.

## Key Features

The youwen5 flake has more correct packaging that closely mirrors how Firefox is packaged in nixpkgs, using wrapFirefox. Update checks are disabled by default through a Firefox policy, as the browser cannot update itself when installed with Nix anyway.

These flakes support multiple systems including x86_64-linux and aarch64-linux, and offer different variants like stable, beta, and twilight releases.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text:
Based on my search results, here are the working Zen Browser Nix flake URLs:

**Most recommended options:**

1. **`github:youwen5/zen-browser-flake`** - This is the most actively maintained. It was forked from the unmaintained MarceColl fork and has better packaging that mirrors how Firefox is packaged in nixpkgs using wrapFirefox. It has daily automatic update checks via GitHub Actions.

2. **`github:0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake`** - A community-driven flake that supports both home-manager and nixpkgs integration.

3. **`github:NikSneMC/zen-browser-flake`** - Exposes three channels (alpha, beta, twilight) and every version of Zen Browser.

**Less recommended:**
- `github:MarceColl/zen-browser-flake` - The original, but now unmaintained (superseded by youwen5's fork)

**Recommendation:** Use **`github:youwen5/zen-browser-flake`** as it's the most actively maintained with automatic daily updates and the best packaging practices.

Sources:
- [GitHub - youwen5/zen-browser-flake](https://github.com/youwen5/zen-browser-flake)
- [GitHub - 0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake](https://github.com/0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake)
- [GitHub - NikSneMC/zen-browser-flake](https://github.com/NikSneMC/zen-browser-flake)

input.command:
gh pr create --title "Add 1Password and Zen browser to desktop config" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Add zen-browser flake input (`0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake`) — manages its own nixpkgs pin
- Enable 1Password CLI and GUI at the system level with polkit policy for the default user
- Add Zen browser to home packages and make it the default browser (MIME associations)
- Update Hyprland `Super+B` keybinding to launch `zen` instead of `google-chrome-stable`
- Replace `chrome-flags.conf` with standalone `electron-flags.conf` (Zen handles Wayland natively)
- Chrome remains installed as a package for optional use

## Test plan
- [ ] `nix flake check` passes (nixosConfigurations eval verified)
- [ ] Rebuild on a desktop host and confirm Zen launches with `Super+B`
- [ ] Verify `xdg-open https://example.com` opens in Zen
- [ ] Verify 1Password GUI launches and prompts for setup
- [ ] Verify Electron apps still respect Wayland flags

Run: 20260401-1153-ab3a
Fixes #402
EOF
)"

content.content:

https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/403

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/403

**Changes made:**
1. **`flake.nix`** — Added `zen-browser` input pointing to `github:0xc000022070/zen-browser-flake` (no `nixpkgs.follows`)
2. **`modules/common/desktop.nix`** — Added `programs._1password` and `programs._1password-gui` with polkit
3. **`home/desktop.nix`** — Added `inputs` arg, added Zen to packages, changed MIME defaults from `google-chrome.desktop` to `zen.desktop`, replaced `chrome-flags.conf` + self-referencing `electron-flags.conf` with standalone `electron-flags.conf`
4. **`home/hyprland.nix`** — Changed `Super+B` from `google-chrome-stable` to `zen`

Chrome is kept in packages. Config eval verified successfully.
